skill.name: firecrawl-build-interact
skill.description: Integrate Firecrawl `/interact` into product code for dynamic pages and browser actions after scraping. Use when a feature needs clicks, form fills, pagination, authentication-aware flows, or other multi-step interactions that plain `/scrape` cannot complete.
skill.summary: Build product integrations with Firecrawl `/interact` for browser automation and dynamic page workflows.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: All 26 static findings are false positives. The SKILL.md file contains only documentation with no executable code. Markdown inline code backticks were misclassified as shell execution, relative Markdown cross-references as path traversal, and YAML configuration fields as credential access and weak cryptography. No security risks were confirmed.

content.user_title: Build Firecrawl `/interact` integrations for dynamic pages
content.value_statement: Scraping static HTML is often not enough when content loads after clicks, form submissions, or logins. This skill guides you to integrate Firecrawl `/interact` so your product can automate browser actions and extract data from dynamic workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guide integration of Firecrawl `/interact` endpoint into product code
Recommend when to escalate from `/scrape` to `/interact`
Suggest scoped browser workflows for clicks, forms, and pagination
Advise on persistent profiles for authenticated sessions
Provide language-specific documentation links for implementation
Outline escalation rules to related Firecrawl skills
limitations: Does not execute browser automation itself; only provides integration guidance
Requires a valid Firecrawl API key for live requests
Not a replacement for a full browser sandbox in open-ended automation cases
Focused on product integration patterns, not general web scraping tutorials

use_cases.title: Automate login-gated data extraction
use_cases.description: Build features that log into dashboards and extract reports by integrating `/interact` for authentication flows.

use_cases.title: Handle paginated search results
use_cases.description: Integrate browser actions to click through paginated or filtered result sets and collect structured data.

use_cases.title: Multi-step form workflows
use_cases.description: Guide implementation of flows that require form fills, dropdown selections, and confirmation clicks before data appears.

prompt_templates.title: Basic `/interact` integration
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to integrate Firecrawl `/interact` into my Node.js product to click a load-more button and scrape the results. Walk me through the setup.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner getting started with browser automation

prompt_templates.title: Escalation from `/scrape` to `/interact`
prompt_templates.prompt: My current `/scrape` integration misses content that loads after a login. When should I switch to `/interact` and what changes are needed?
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer deciding between scrape and interact

prompt_templates.title: Scoped workflow design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a minimal `/interact` workflow for my Python app: log in, navigate to the orders page, and extract the table. Keep the interaction scope as small as possible.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user optimizing browser workflow scope

prompt_templates.title: Persistent session integration
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to reuse an authenticated browser session across multiple `/interact` calls in my product. How do I configure persistent profiles with Firecrawl?
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user managing authenticated state across sessions

output_examples.input: How do I integrate Firecrawl `/interact` for a paginated search flow in TypeScript?
output: 2. Initialize the client with your API key.
3. Define an `/interact` action array: click the next button, wait for results to load, then scrape.
4. Loop the action array until pagination ends or your limit is reached.
5. Parse and store the structured data from each page response.

output_examples.input: When should I use `/interact` instead of `/scrape`?
output: Use `/scrape` when the target page renders all content immediately in the HTML.
Escalate to `/interact` when content requires clicks, typing, navigation, or authentication before it appears.
Keep `/interact` scoped to the smallest set of actions that unlock the data you need.

best_practices: Start with `/scrape` and only escalate to `/interact` when dynamic behavior is required
Scope `/interact` workflows to the minimum actions needed to unlock target data
Use persistent browser profiles only when authenticated state must persist across sessions
anti_patterns: Using `/interact` for every page when `/scrape` would suffice
Building overly broad browser automation without evaluating a dedicated sandbox
Hardcoding credentials inside interaction scripts instead of using secure secret management

faq.question: What is the difference between `/scrape` and `/interact`?
faq.answer: `/scrape` reads a page as-is. `/interact` performs browser actions such as clicks and form fills before extracting content.

faq.question: Do I need a Firecrawl API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill guides integration, but live requests to Firecrawl require a valid `FIRECRAWL_API_KEY`.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with self-hosted Firecrawl?
faq.answer: Yes. You can set `FIRECRAWL_API_URL` to point to your self-hosted deployment.

faq.question: Which programming languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill links to official documentation for Node, Python, Rust, Java, Elixir, and cURL.

faq.question: Is `/interact` suitable for fully open-ended browser automation?
faq.answer: Not always. If the use case is fully open-ended, evaluate whether a dedicated browser sandbox is a better fit.

faq.question: How do I keep `/interact` workflows secure?
faq.answer: Scope actions tightly, avoid hardcoding secrets, and reuse persistent profiles only when necessary.
